behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Candidate Integration test: stub STT response with correct speaker tags and verify downstream attribution wiring; this only catches mapping bugs. Candidate Agentic test: run real meeting audio through the real STT model and assert speaker labels are preserved and stable enough for attribution; this catches diarization/model errors that stubs hide (MOCK-HIDE, LLM-DEP). Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets one model boundary (STT label fidelity), not a multi-agentic sequence as the primary failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Use real multi-speaker audio with known speaker turns, call the real STT API, then verify transcript speaker labels align to attendees well enough that attribution fields can be populated correctly.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Candidate Unit test: feed representative transcript text into parser and assert assignee/task pairs in output structure; catches parsing/regex/AST logic failures directly. Candidate Integration test: wire parser with surrounding services; adds little for this behavior because failure mode is parser logic itself, not module handoff. Behavior text is deterministic transformation and does not require real infra or model output, so lower level is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven parser tests over transcript snippets (clear, ambiguous, malformed assignment phrasing) asserting exact extracted assignee and task text fields.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Candidate Integration test: mock summarizer output and verify validator/check logic enforces attendee coverage; catches only wrapper logic. Candidate Agentic test: submit real transcripts to real LLM summarization and assert each assigned attendee appears with >=1 action item; catches omission/attribution failures caused by model judgment (LLM-DEP, deterministic-assertion trap). Rejected Workflow because the core failure boundary is summarizer decision quality for this condition, not multiple distinct agentic decisions chained as the thing being tested.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcripts with known per-attendee assignments through the production summarization prompt/model and assert the structured action_items include at least one item per assigned attendee.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Candidate Unit test: test only a mapper function that writes decisions to schema; catches local mapping bugs. Candidate Integration test: run extractor output through routing/serialization modules and assert identified decisions land in `decisions` and not narrative; catches contract/handoff mismatches between modules (MOCK-MISS). INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: no real OS/network/subprocess needed, no dependence on real model quality, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic transcript fixtures with known decisions, execute extractor + schema-router together in-process, and assert decision objects are emitted only under `decisions` fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Candidate Integration test: stub LLM output already containing proper decisions placement; only validates post-processing. Candidate Agentic test: call real LLM with real transcript text and assert key decisions are surfaced in `decisions` section; catches model extraction/placement failures hidden by stubs (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow because behavior text centers on one live summarization outcome, not a required multi-step agentic pipeline criterion.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate real-model summaries for transcripts with gold key decisions, scoring whether each gold decision appears in the structured `decisions` section instead of freeform narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Candidate Agentic test: one segment through STT+LLM validates single-step model behavior only. Candidate Workflow test: real >60-minute audio triggers segmentation, multiple sequential STT/LLM passes, and merge coherence checks; catches cross-step context loss/order drift/merge regressions that lower levels miss (MULTI-STEP). Rejected System because behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-segment processing and coherent merged summary, which depends on multiple agentic outputs across steps.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real long recording (>60 min), run full segmented processing in production order, and assert all segments are processed, merged summary is structurally valid, and cross-segment action items/decisions remain coherent and non-duplicative.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Candidate System test: verify endpoint/subprocess chain works with mocks for model responses; catches transport/orchestration faults only. Candidate Workflow test: real recording through real STT and real LLM to final delivery, asserting autonomous completion; catches failures from combined agentic decisions and stage handoffs that mocked runs hide (REAL-INFRA + LLM-DEP + MULTI-STEP). Rejected Agentic because behavior text explicitly requires end-to-end multi-stage completion, not a single model decision boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like end-to-end job on a real meeting file (upload to delivered structured payload), with no human edits, and assert successful completion plus schema-valid output containing action items, decisions, and attendee attributions.
